---
title: "Line list and contacts generation"
subtitle: "Reproducing the simulist R package functionality"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
engine: julia
---

## Overview

Simulated line lists and contact tracing data are generated here,
reproducing the core functionality of the
[**simulist**](https://github.com/epiverse-trace/simulist) R package.

```{julia}
using EpiBranch
using Distributions
using DataFrames
using Dates
using Random: Xoshiro
```

## Basic line list

Simulate an outbreak with demographics and clinical transitions, then
project to a line list. `linelist` itself is a pure projection — all
randomness lives in `attributes` and `transitions`:

```{julia}
model = BranchingProcess(Poisson(1.5), LogNormal(1.6, 0.5))

attrs = compose(
    clinical_presentation(incubation_period = LogNormal(1.5, 0.5)),
    demographics(age_distribution = Normal(40, 15), prob_female = 0.55),
)

transitions = [
    Reporting(delay = Exponential(3.0)),
    Hospitalisation(delay = Exponential(5.0), probability = 0.2),
    Death(delay = Exponential(14.0), probability = 0.05),
    Recovery(delay = Exponential(14.0)),
]

rng = Xoshiro(42)
state = simulate(model;
    condition = 50:200,
    attributes = attrs,
    transitions = transitions,
    sim_opts = SimOpts(max_cases = 200),
    rng = rng,
)

ll = linelist(state; reference_date = Date(2024, 1, 1))
println("Line list: $(nrow(ll)) cases, $(ncol(ll)) columns")
first(ll, 5)
```

## Demographics

Demographics live in the `attributes` layer via the
[`demographics`](@ref) builder — see the line list above. To inspect:

```{julia}
println("Age range: $(minimum(ll.age)) - $(maximum(ll.age))")
println("Female proportion: $(round(count(==("female"), ll.sex) / nrow(ll), digits=2))")
```

## Age-stratified case fatality risk

Age-conditional CFR is a closure on `Death.probability`, reading
`ind.state[:age]`:

```{julia}
cfr_by_age = ind -> begin
    age = ind.state[:age]
    age <= 14 ? 0.001 : age <= 64 ? 0.01 : 0.15
end

age_stratified = [
    Reporting(delay = Exponential(3.0)),
    Hospitalisation(delay = Exponential(5.0), probability = 0.2),
    Death(delay = Exponential(14.0),
        probability = (rng, ind) -> cfr_by_age(ind)),
    Recovery(delay = Exponential(14.0)),
]

rng = Xoshiro(42)
state_age = simulate(model;
    condition = 50:200,
    attributes = attrs,
    transitions = age_stratified,
    sim_opts = SimOpts(max_cases = 200),
    rng = rng,
)

ll_age_cfr = linelist(state_age; reference_date = Date(2024, 1, 1))

for (lo, hi) in [(0, 14), (15, 64), (65, 90)]
    group = filter(r -> lo <= r.age <= hi, ll_age_cfr)
    n_died = count(==("died"), group.outcome)
    pct = round(n_died / max(nrow(group), 1) * 100, digits = 1)
    println("Age $lo-$hi: $(nrow(group)) cases, $n_died deaths ($pct%)")
end
```

## Contacts table

Both infected and non-infected contacts are included in the contacts
table, with a `was_case` flag:

```{julia}
ct = contacts(state; reference_date = Date(2024, 1, 1))
println("Total contacts: ", nrow(ct))
println("Infected contacts (was_case=true): ", count(ct.was_case))
println("Non-infected contacts: ", count(.!ct.was_case))
first(ct, 5)
```

## Conditioning on outbreak size

Use rejection sampling to generate outbreaks of a specific size:

```{julia}
rng = Xoshiro(42)
state_small = simulate(model;
    condition = 30:100,
    attributes = attrs,
    sim_opts = SimOpts(max_cases = 200),
    rng = rng,
)

println("Outbreak size: ", state_small.cumulative_cases, " (target: 30-100)")
```

## Combined outbreak simulation

Generate both line list and contacts from the same simulation:

```{julia}
rng = Xoshiro(42)
model = BranchingProcess(NegBin(1.5, 0.5), LogNormal(1.6, 0.5))
state = simulate(model;
    condition = 50:200,
    attributes = attrs,
    transitions = transitions,
    sim_opts = SimOpts(max_cases = 200, n_initial = 3),
    rng = rng,
)

ref_date = Date(2024, 1, 1)
ll = linelist(state; reference_date = ref_date)
ct = contacts(state; reference_date = ref_date)
cs = chain_statistics(state)

println("Line list: $(nrow(ll)) cases")
println("Contacts: $(nrow(ct)) contact events ($(count(ct.was_case)) infected)")
println("Chains: $(nrow(cs)) chains")
println("Chain sizes: ", cs.size)
```
